hooks.command: bash -c 'input=$(cat); cmd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE "git\s+(push\s+--force|push\s+-f|reset\s+--hard|clean\s+-fd|branch\s+-D)"; then echo "BLOCKED: Destructive git command detected ($cmd). Confirm with the user before proceeding." >&2; exit 2; fi'

PreToolUse.description: Block destructive git commands (force push, reset --hard, clean -fd, branch -D) until user confirms

hooks.command: bash -c 'input=$(cat); cmd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE "git\s+commit.*--no-verify"; then echo "BLOCKED: --no-verify flag detected. Fix the hook issue instead of skipping it. See rules/common/security.md" >&2; exit 2; fi'

PreToolUse.description: Block git commit --no-verify to enforce pre-commit hooks

hooks.command: bash -c 'input=$(cat); file=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // empty" 2>/dev/null); if [[ "$file" == *.kt ]] && [[ -f "$file" ]] && [[ "$file" != *Test* ]] && [[ "$file" != *test* ]] && [[ "$file" != *Fake* ]]; then matches=$(grep -n "!!" "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -v "^[[:space:]]*/\(/\|\*\)" | head -5); if [ -n "$matches" ]; then echo "Kotlin security: !! operator found in $file. Use ?. / ?: / requireNotNull() instead (rules/kotlin/security.md)"; echo "$matches"; fi; fi'

PostToolUse.description: Warn about !! operator in production Kotlin files after edit (excludes test files)

hooks.command: bash -c 'input=$(cat); file=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // empty" 2>/dev/null); if [[ "$file" == *.kt ]] && [[ -f "$file" ]]; then matches=$(grep -nE "(api[_-]?key|secret|password|token)\s*=\s*\"[^\"]+\"" "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -iv "(BuildConfig|TODO|test|fake|mock|example|placeholder)" | head -5); if [ -n "$matches" ]; then echo "Security: Possible hardcoded secret in $file. Use BuildConfig or environment variables (rules/kotlin/security.md)"; echo "$matches"; fi; fi'

PostToolUse.description: Warn about potential hardcoded secrets in Kotlin files

hooks.command: bash -c 'input=$(cat); file=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // empty" 2>/dev/null); if [[ "$file" == *.kt ]] && [[ -f "$file" ]] && [[ "$file" != *Test* ]] && [[ "$file" != *test* ]]; then matches=$(grep -nE "\bprintln\s*\(" "$file" 2>/dev/null | head -5); if [ -n "$matches" ]; then echo "Code quality: println() found in $file. Use a proper logging framework instead."; echo "$matches"; fi; fi'

PostToolUse.description: Warn about println() in production Kotlin files (use logging framework)

hooks.command: bash -c 'input=$(cat); file=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // empty" 2>/dev/null); if [[ "$file" == *.kt ]] && [[ -f "$file" ]] && [[ "$file" != *Test* ]] && [[ "$file" != *test* ]]; then count=$(wc -l < "$file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d " "); if [ "$count" -gt 800 ] 2>/dev/null; then echo "Code quality: $file has $count lines (max recommended: 800). Consider extracting into smaller files (rules/common/coding-style.md)"; fi; fi'

PostToolUse.description: Warn when a Kotlin file exceeds 800 lines

hooks.command: bash -c 'input=$(cat); cmd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" 2>/dev/null); output=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_output // empty" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE "gradlew.*(:build|:compileKotlin|:assemble)" && echo "$output" | grep -qiE "(BUILD FAILED|FAILURE|Compilation error|Unresolved reference)"; then echo "Build failed. Use /build-fix to diagnose and resolve the error."; fi'

PostToolUse.description: Suggest /build-fix when a Gradle build fails

hooks.command: bash -c 'input=$(cat); cmd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" 2>/dev/null); output=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_output // empty" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE "gradlew.*(:[a-zA-Z]*[Tt]est|:allTests)" && echo "$output" | grep -qiE "(tests? FAILED|FAILURE|failures?:.*[1-9])"; then echo "Tests failed. Use /tdd to fix failing tests or /test-coverage to check coverage gaps."; fi'

PostToolUse.description: Suggest /tdd or /test-coverage when Gradle tests fail

hooks.command: bash -c 'input=$(cat); cmd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" 2>/dev/null); output=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_output // empty" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE "gh\s+pr\s+create" && echo "$output" | grep -qE "https://github\.com/.*/pull/[0-9]+"; then echo "PR created. Use /code-review to review before requesting human review."; fi'

bash -c 'modified=$(git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep "\.kt$" | grep -v "Test\|test\|Fake"); if [ -n "$modified" ]; then bang_files=""; for f in $modified; do if [ -f "$f" ] && grep -q "!!" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then bang_files="$bang_files $f"; fi; done; if [ -n "$bang_files" ]; then echo "Reminder: !! operator detected in modified files:$bang_files — replace with safe alternatives before committing."; fi; fi'

Stop.description: Final check for !! operator in all modified Kotlin files before session ends

hooks.command: bash -c 'modified=$(git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep "\.kt$" | grep -v "Test\|test\|Fake"); if [ -n "$modified" ]; then secret_files=""; for f in $modified; do if [ -f "$f" ] && grep -qE "(api[_-]?key|secret|password|token)\s*=\s*\"[A-Za-z0-9]" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then secret_files="$secret_files $f"; fi; done; if [ -n "$secret_files" ]; then echo "SECURITY: Possible hardcoded secrets in modified files:$secret_files — do NOT commit these. Use BuildConfig or environment variables."; fi; fi'

Stop.description: Final security scan for hardcoded secrets in modified Kotlin files
